applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused HardSwish + GroupNorm + Mean kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented a 3-kernel Triton approach: (1) compute per-channel HardSwish sum/sum_sq, (2) reduce to group-level mean/variance, (3) apply GroupNorm and compute output mean. Each kernel uses grid-stride loops over spatial dimensions with BLOCK_SIZE=1024.

attempted_but_failed.name: Single kernel with nested Python loops
attempted_but_failed.reason: Python loops over spatial dimensions (16*32*32=16384 iterations) were extremely slow

attempted_but_failed.name: BLOCK_SIZE=2048
attempted_but_failed.reason: Larger block size reduced parallelism and increased runtime

notes: Conv3d is already optimized by MIOpen (75% of runtime) - cannot improve further
The remaining 25% (HardSwish + GroupNorm + Mean) was fused into Triton kernels
3-kernel approach is necessary because GroupNorm requires group-level statistics that depend on all channels in a group
Triton kernel launch overhead and grid-stride loop inefficiency limit further optimization

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output — the agent has failed 3 consecutive trials. With 39 minutes left, giving one final attempt with extremely prescriptive copy-paste-ready instructions focused on torch.compil

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 failed with no output — the 4th consecutive failure. The agent keeps getting stuck trying complex optimizations. With 39 minutes left, providing ultra-minimal instructions to at least recover

global_notes: KernelBench Level 2 Problem 27: Conv3d + HardSwish + GroupNorm + Mean pooling
Conv3d dominates runtime (75%) and is already optimized by MIOpen
Fused Triton kernel for post-Conv operations achieves modest 3.4% speedup
Further optimization would require fusing Conv3d itself, which is not practical with Triton
